name: AI-Powered Testing
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: GPT-4o for test generation, mutation analysis, and failure root-cause reasoning

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for parallel test generation across repos

services.name: Container Apps
services.purpose: Test generation engine — code parsing, mutation injection, coverage analysis, API gateway

services.name: Azure Container Registry
services.purpose: Docker images for sandbox test execution environments per language runtime

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Test results storage, mutation scores, coverage history, repository metadata

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Source code snapshots, generated test files, coverage reports, mutation logs

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: GitHub tokens, OpenAI API keys, repository access credentials

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Test generation metrics, mutation detection rates, CI pipeline latency tracking

services.name: Log Analytics
services.purpose: Centralized logging — generation runs, sandbox executions, GitHub webhook events

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini for boilerplate unit test generation — reserve GPT-4o for complex integration test reasoning and mutation analysis
Cache mutation analysis results per function signature — skip re-analysis for unchanged code between CI runs
Use Container Apps scale-to-zero for dev — test generation workloads are bursty and align well with consumption pricing
Implement per-repo token budgets to prevent cost spikes from large monorepo test generation sweeps
Use Cosmos DB serverless for dev — saves $45/month vs provisioned for intermittent test result storage
Deduplicate generated tests against existing test suites before execution — reduces sandbox compute costs by 30-50%
